title: salix cheilophila
intro: Salix cheilophila, also known as the white willow, is a small shrub or tree that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds, such as salicin, which is similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix cheilophila include reducing fever, relieving headaches and joint pain, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
